name: ERG (absolute-watt workout)
version: PerfPRO / TrainerRoad-era plain-text ERG course format.
spec: ERG is a plain-text format with two sections. [COURSE HEADER] holds metadata as KEY = VALUE lines, notably VERSION, UNITS = ENGLISH|METRIC, DESCRIPTION, FILE NAME, MINUTES WATTS (the column legend). [COURSE DATA] is whitespace-separated rows of 'minutes watts' — elapsed time in (fractional) minutes and the absolute target power in watts at that time. Power between points is interpolated linearly, so a step change is encoded by two rows at the same minute with different watts. The file is FTP-independent only in the sense that watts are absolute; recreating %FTP requires knowing the rider FTP the file was authored for.

examples.title: Steady 200 W for 20 min
examples.content:
[COURSE HEADER]
VERSION = 2
UNITS = ENGLISH
DESCRIPTION = Steady
MINUTES WATTS
[END COURSE HEADER]
[COURSE DATA]
0.00	200
20.00	200
[END COURSE DATA]

examples.title: Step from 150 to 250 W
examples.content:
[COURSE HEADER]
VERSION = 2
UNITS = ENGLISH
MINUTES WATTS
[END COURSE HEADER]
[COURSE DATA]
0.00	150
10.00	150
10.00	250
20.00	250
[END COURSE DATA]

examples.title: Linear ramp 150->300 W
examples.content:
[COURSE HEADER]
VERSION = 2
UNITS = ENGLISH
MINUTES WATTS
[END COURSE HEADER]
[COURSE DATA]
0.00	150
25.00	300
[END COURSE DATA]

constraints.note: Watts are absolute; the same file is harder/easier for riders of different FTP.
A vertical step needs two rows sharing the same minute value.
Times are cumulative minutes from the start, not per-segment durations.

conversion_notes.note: Divide each watt value by FTP and multiply by 100 to get %FTP; otherwise the row structure is identical.
Group interpolated rows into Warmup/SteadyState/Ramp steps; convert watts to FTP fractions (watts/FTP).

glossary.definition: The section holding 'minutes watts' rows defining the power-time profile.
Power between two rows is a straight line; identical-minute rows create instantaneous steps.
